stories.title: Make EnsureOnboarding emit honest monitor/log action results
stories.scope: Refactor the backend single-device onboarding ensure path so it no longer fabricates a one-line syslog success. The ensure result must describe both monitor and log onboarding actions when applicable, respect explicit server monitor mode requirements, and record failed/unknown/blocked evidence when a real execution contract or template is missing instead of silently returning success.
nonGoals: Do not implement full SNMP trap delivery.
Do not guess remote success when the backend lacks a concrete contract.
acceptance: EnsureOnboarding no longer returns a hardcoded syslog success as the default happy path.
The response and stored onboarding summary can include both monitor and log actions when the device requires them.
Server monitor mode requirements remain explicit and affect the ensured action set instead of being ignored after planning.
When a concrete runtime contract is missing, the stored onboarding action records failed, unknown, or blocked evidence with an explicit reason instead of fabricated success.
Backend tests cover at least one honest non-success execution path and one server-monitor-mode-sensitive path.
notes: Current gap anchor: DeviceV2API.EnsureOnboarding still synthesizes a log success action and does not make the monitor side honest.
Use the existing onboarding action/evidence contract instead of inventing a new persistence model.

stories.title: Show non-success onboarding evidence clearly in DeviceV2ManagementRedesign
stories.scope: Harden the redesign execute flow so the actual start/ensure path does not feel like generic success when the backend reports failed, unknown, blocked, or retryable actions. The UI should surface backend action evidence, keep explicit monitor-mode requirements visible, and avoid unconditional success messaging after ensure returns non-success action states.
nonGoals: Do not redesign unrelated Device V2 panels.
Do not add batch execution UX.
Do not hide backend action details behind a generic success toast.
acceptance: The redesign execute flow does not show a generic success-only outcome when onboarding actions contain failed, unknown, or blocked items.
Retryable and non-success actions are surfaced from backend evidence after ensure returns.
Server monitor mode requirements remain explicit on the execute path and are not lost between plan and ensure.
The UI keeps the single-device-only rule and still renders backend action evidence directly.
notes: Current gap anchor: the execute path always raises a success toast once ensure returns, even though the backend evidence model already distinguishes non-success states.

stories.title: Capture honest execute-path evidence for the next round
stories.scope: Run the intended bounded validation for the honest execute path and write durable evidence for this next round. Validate the backend/frontend contract changes with tests and, when safe within the current local environment, capture the resulting onboarding evidence or a precise blocker without pretending the execution side is already fully closed.
nonGoals: Do not broaden into a platform-wide template rollout.
Do not claim real remote onboarding success without durable evidence.
acceptance: A durable evidence summary is written for the honest execute-path round.
final-readiness.md and review.md reflect whether the new round reduced the real execute-path gap or exposed a concrete remaining blocker.
The evidence clearly distinguishes local contract/test validation from any real side-effectful onboarding execution.
notes: This story is the bounded validation/evidence close-out for batch-004, not a claim that the whole onboarding domain is complete.
